CROSS-DEFENDANT STEPHEN FONG'S DEPOSITION SHALL GO FORWARD ON JANUARY 29, 2013 SUBJECT TO THE FOLLOWING TERMS AND CONDITIONS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2025.420(B): 1) THE DURATION OF THE DEPOSITION SHALL BE LIMITED TO 3 HOURS ON THE RECORD; 2) THE DEPOSITION MAY BE TAKEN OVER MORE THAN ONE DAY, INCLUDING WEEKENDS; 3) THE DEPOSITION SHALL BE TAKEN AT A LOCATION TO BE DETERMINED BY CROSS-DEFENDANT STEPHEN FONG. Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SYBIL RENICK OF THE PRATHER LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 881-7774 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CAITLIN M. EMMETT OF THE BLEDSOE LLP, TEL. NO. 981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Chinese Hospital Association's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiff's proposed amendment adding a cause of action for conversion arises out of the same set of facts as alleged in the First Amended complaint. Defendant will not be meaningfully prejudiced by the addition of this cause of action. This order is without prejudice to a motion to continue trial being filed in Department 206. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Pltf Complaint As A Whole Ane Each Of The Causes Of Action Therein
Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Sybil Renick (415-881-7774) apperaing for plaintiff and opposing party / Atty. Jeffrey Ta (415-981-5411) appearing for cross complainant and moving party.

Argued - The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: Cross-Complainant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED as follows:

1. Judgment on the Pleadings is granted as to the first cause of action for specific performance with leave to amend to allege bargained-for consideration promised prior to and in exchange for donation; and all elements of enforceable gift, including "complete gift where nothing gift left undone" in light of paragraph 2 of Assignment.
2. GRANTED without leave to amend as to causes of action for unjust enrichment and constructive trust.
3.GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts showing detrimental reliance and harm.

Plaintiff shall have 10 days leave to amend from the Notice of Entry of order. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Harry Dorfman, Clerk: N. Bautista, Not reported (D501)
